$comment: DECLARE-ONLY — NOT APPLIED (TIN-2541). Route INTENT for GFTB on-cluster web serving, mirroring the shape of blahaj:tofu/intent/massageithaca/public-edge-routes.json but fail-closed: every enablement flag is false, and the tunnel public-hostname route is Cloudflare dashboard/token-managed (TIN-991), NOT created from this file. This is the PRIVATE overlay apply-plane intent; the public site repo carries ZERO route/endpoint/secret material. No live tunnel UUID or credential is inlined here — the real cfargotunnel target and zone binding live in the operator-gated edge stack (tofu/stacks/edge*) and the cloudflared dashboard.

tunnel.name: honey-ingress
tunnel.note: The honey-ingress tunnel is the shared house connector (blahaj:deploy/honey/retained-cloudflared.yaml). Its public-hostname routes are dashboard/token-managed (TIN-991). The real <tunnel-id>.cfargotunnel.com target is NOT inlined here; an operator adds the route out of band at cutover.

authority.route_mutation_policy: operator-gated; no CI auto-applier for durable single-service routes (TIN-991)
authority.cloudflare_credential: protected `edge` environment only; never in this repo tree, never in the public site repo

planned_route.$comment: What a cutover WOULD declare. Present for review only; enable_* stays false until a superseding hosting ADR + a live pod-headroom probe authorize it.
planned_route.tls: terminates at the Cloudflare edge (proxied); origin hop tunnel->Service is plain in-cluster HTTP, exactly as MI

access.posture: gate is dashboard-managed; declare-only skeleton mints no Access app

supersedes_nothing: ADR 0003 (site repo) keeps static production on CF Pages. This route intent is the fail-closed skeleton a superseding on-cluster hosting ADR (TIN-2537 research brief) would build on; it changes no live posture.
